title: Review: full-project third-pass
notes:
Maintainer risk: LOW — only two new findings, both about error message quality rather than correctness. The core security properties (zeroization, input validation, URI injection prevention) are intact. The slash-in-secret error message will generate user confusion; the body-reading gap is a debugging convenience.
Confidence: 95% chance of no missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: azure-kv and bitwarden/gcp-sm/doppler are unit-tests-only (not integration-tested against live services). Would raise confidence if: integration tests ran against live Azure KV, GCP SM, and Doppler. The correctness of error-mapping, credential chains, and SDK behavior cannot be verified without live services.
